executiveSummary.text: Grafton Group Plc (GFTU.L) is now back in sync with the canonical DYOR HQ sheet after the metadata repair pass. The current stance is REDUCE with conviction 50/100, using N/A as the latest stored price reference where available.

businessModel.text: Grafton Group Plc is exposed to repair, remodel and non-residential building activity, with returns shaped by volume discipline, pricing and product mix. The rebuilt note now matches the corrected company mapping.

recentCatalysts.text: Housing and renovation demand, infrastructure activity, mix improvement and cost recovery are the main operating levers.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case: the core operating backdrop remains good enough to support the present reduce stance, but not strong enough to justify a looser risk view without better evidence. Bull case: execution and demand improve faster than expected. Bear case: operating or funding pressure worsens and compresses valuation further.

keyRisks.text: Construction activity can soften in a weaker macro backdrop. Input-cost inflation can pressure margins. Volumes can fall faster than pricing adjusts. Acquisition or integration execution can disappoint. Channel destocking can create noisy quarters.

risks.risk: Construction activity can soften in a weaker macro backdrop.
Volumes can fall faster than pricing adjusts.
Acquisition or integration execution can disappoint.
Channel destocking can create noisy quarters.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Investors who want cyclical building-products exposure with operating leverage. Avoid: Investors seeking defensive earnings and low macro sensitivity.

recommendation.text: REDUCE - Conviction: 50/100. This rebuilt page is intentionally concise, but it is now internally coherent and aligned to the corrected ticker and company metadata.

entryExit.text: Keep position sizing consistent with a reduce stance until fuller company-specific analysis is refreshed.
